title: Unit, Property, Replay, Headless SDL, Render, and Playtest Evidence · Transfer Build
summary: An independent adaptive-arcade team receives a different viewport, device, frame rate, seed, entity count, accessibility profile, platform, and delivery constraint and transfers unit, property, replay, headless sdl, render, and playtest evidence into a changed risk-layered automated and human game test suite. Planned artifact: Produce an independent changed-context version of risk-layered automated and human game test suite with automated and judgment evidence. Learners progress through predict…
objectives: Test vector, wrap, time, input, state, score, collision, spawn, and migration invariants with fixed fixtures.
Generate bounded cases for wrap, collision symmetry, conservation, reconciliation, replay, and invalid input and retain smallest failures.
Use dummy video and audio drivers for adapter integration while labeling missing window, GPU, timing, device, and sensory evidence.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-asteroids-python cumulative accessible real-time game portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python simulation models and fixed fixtures only.
Native pygame-ce, SDL, windows, controllers, audio devices, assets, files, installers,
GPU behavior, frame pacing, accessibility playtests, packaging, and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class SimulationPolicy:
    fixed_step_seconds: float = 1 / 120
    max_frame_seconds: float = 0.1
    max_updates_per_frame: int = 8
    logical_width: int = 960
    logical_height: int = 540


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class RunEvidence:
    revision: str
    seed: int
    input_trace_id: str
    runtime_id: str
    accessibility_profile: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python Asteroids portfolio ready"

steps.title: Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Commit to the mission model · Lab Pure Unit Tests
steps.instruction:
lab case — A lab Asteroids team handles case 3769 during a headless continuous-integration replay. Build deterministic Python evidence to layer pure unit and property tests, deterministic replay, headless SDL integration, render contracts, changed defects, and manual accessibility playtests; retain player goal, revision, Python, pygame-ce, SDL, fixture, seed, input trace, viewport, and result identity; then run the packaged build with dummy video and audio drivers, missing optional sound, and a corrupted save. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real pygame-ce, SDL, windows, controllers, audio devices, assets, files, installers, frame timing, GPUs, packaging, and production behavior require explicit controlled desktop transfer gates.

What must be established before implementation?

Evidence target: Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Commit to the mission model · Lab Pure Unit Tests.

For Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Commit to the mission model · Lab Pure Unit Tests, keep Pure Unit Tests connected to executable Python game-state, timing, input, collision, accessibility, test, package, and release decision evidence.

steps.title: Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Extend the cumulative artifact · Lab Playtest Accessibility
steps.instruction:
Append a runnable pure-Python function headed "# Evidence: asteroids-playtest-accessibility-1axj6f" that uses case 5885 to layer pure unit and property tests, deterministic replay, headless SDL integration, render contracts, changed defects, and manual accessibility playtests. Keep its defaults runnable, assert an invariant, and return observable changed-case evidence. Browser code must not claim native pygame-ce, SDL, window, controller, mixer, filesystem, package, GPU, installer, or production behavior; verify those boundaries later with Python 3.14.6, released pygame-ce 2.5.7, fixed and headless SDL fixtures, real devices, licensed assets, accessibility playtests, profiling, clean installations, rollback, recovery, and release gates.

content.code:
# Evidence: asteroids-playtest-accessibility-1axj6f
# Competency: asteroids-playtest-accessibility.
# Case 5885: a low-vision laptop playtest at 125 percent scaling.
# Operating constraint: keep browser work pure, deterministic, bounded, and free of native display, device, audio, file, package, and operating-system effects.
# Changed case: replace an audio-only collision cue with synchronized text, shape, and optional sound evidence.
def evidence_asteroids_playtest_accessibility_1axj6f(unit_cases: int = 24, property_cases: int = 40, headless_cases: int = 9, changed_defect_detected: bool = True, dummy_driver_verified: bool = True, render_contract_checked: bool = True) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_1axj6f = "1axj6f-f6jxa1-5885"
    assert evidence_variant_1axj6f
    evidence_axes_1axj6f = (
        "player1axj6f",
        "seed5885",
        "inputf6jxa1",
        "frame1axj6f5885",
        "state58851axj6f",
        "playtestf6jxa15885",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_1axj6f)) == 6
    if min(unit_cases, property_cases, headless_cases) < 1:
        return False, unit_cases, "test-layers"
    if not changed_defect_detected:
        return False, unit_cases, "mutation-survived"
    if not dummy_driver_verified:
        return False, headless_cases, "headless-gap"
    if not render_contract_checked:
        return False, headless_cases, "render-gap"
    total = unit_cases + property_cases + headless_cases
    assert total >= 3
    return True, total, "risk-layered-game-tests"
